description: Architect's verdict-half output. Two ballots — vote accept or reject on every lead correction in scope and on every role-scoped rule the leads have accepted (this iteration's freshly raised + still-accepted from earlier rounds). Silence is not a vote; voting per item is what surfaces silent drift on rules previously accepted.

corrections.description: One vote per lead correction in scope (corrections sitting in <role>.corrections.yaml after lead_round). Vote on every entry; skipping is not a vote.

id.description: The correction's id, copied verbatim (e.g. v84-1.frontend.pages.c.3 or v84-1.backend.lead.c.1).

reason.description: Required when verdict=reject. One short prose line naming the cross-role conflict — cite the conflicting role / action_id, or the responsibility encroached.

rules.description: One vote per role-scoped rule the leads have accepted in this iteration (freshly raised + still-accepted from earlier rounds). Vote on every entry; skipping is not a vote.

id.description: The rule's id, copied verbatim (e.g. v84-1.frontend.lead.rule.3 or v84-1.frontend.pages.rule.2).

reason.description: Required when verdict=reject. One short prose line naming the cross-role conflict — cite the conflicting global / role rule id, the role that cannot honor the contract, or the responsibility encroached.

examples.title: Mixed verdicts — most accept, a few reject for distinct cross-role breaks

corrections.reason: Tells backend to expose internal job ids on the wire, but frontend draft v84-1.3.frontend.4 expects opaque slugs per global v84-0.global.rule.5.

rules.reason: Mandates client-side caching of API responses; conflicts with global v84-0.global.rule.4 which requires every API read to hit the server for freshness.
Requires synchronous job dispatch on every write, but devops layout pins the job runner to an async queue per v84-0.devops.rule.1.

examples.title: All accepts — leads' work stands cross-role
